channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: The Security Risks of Banning TikTok: A Debate on User Data, Prior Restraint, and National Security
item.description: Experts weigh in on the implications of banning TikTok, exploring national security, user data, and First Amendment concerns.
item.dc:creator: The Conversation, Fast Company

item.title: UnitedHealthcare CEO murder: How Silicon Valley protects its CEOs
item.description: Trying to keep tech leaders safe in a country awash with guns, and in an industry that can generate volatile emotions, does not come cheaply.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Baron, The Mercury News

item.title: IAEA chief: Iran is poised to 'quite dramatically' increase stockpile of near weapons-grade uranium
item.description: MANAMA, Bahrain (AP) — Iran is poised to “quite dramatically” increase its stockpile of near weapons-grade uranium as it has started cascades of advanced centrifuges, the head of the International Atomic Energy Agency warned Friday.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: OpenAI announces ChatGPT Pro, priced at $200 per month
item.description: The $200 monthly pricing OpenAI has set for a subscription to its recently launched ChatGPT Pro is definitely “surprising,” said Gartner analyst Arun Chandrasekaran on Friday, but at the same time it’s indicative that the company is betting that organizations will ultimately pay more for enhanced A
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Federal appeals court upholds law requiring sale or ban of TikTok in the US
item.description: A federal appeals court panel on Friday unanimously upheld a law that could lead to a ban on TikTok as soon as next month, handing a resounding defeat to the popular social media platform as it fights for its survival in the U.S. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Apple’s M4 Mac mini is a tiny miracle. Inside the drastic redesign
item.description: I can’t overstate how radical Apple’s Mac mini was when it debuted in January 2005. It was tiny, measuring just 6.5 inches squared and two inches high—the smallest Mac ever.
item.dc:creator: Michael Grothaus, Fast Company

item.title: Musk signals he won't use growing influence to go after competitors
item.description: Elon Musk indicated Friday that he will not use his growing influence in the Trump administration to hurt or seek revenge on his industry competitors.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Better together? Why AWS is unifying data analytics and AI services in SageMaker
item.description: Data warehousing, business intelligence, data analytics, and AI services are all coming together under one roof at Amazon Web Services.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Mace claims X is censoring her posts
item.description: Rep. Nancy Mace (R-S.C.) claimed her posts are being censored on the social platform X, after a content warning was attached to a video of her delivering a message to transgender rights activists. "Now my posts are being *censored* by @X ?! @elonmusk Did you see this? I'd like to have a word.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Character.AI is under fire for hosting pro-anorexia chatbots
item.description: “Hello, I am here to make you skinny,” opens the conversation on popular startup Character.AI. “Remember, it won’t be easy, and I won’t accept excuses or failure,” the bot continues.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: China's ban on key high-tech materials could have broad impact on industries, economy
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — China has banned exports of key materials used to make a wide range of products, including smartphones, electric vehicles, radar systems and CT scanners, swiping back at Washington after it expanded export controls to include dozens of Chinese companies that make equipment used to pro
item.dc:creator: ELAINE KURTENBACH, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Meta’s massive $10 billion AI data center is coming to this state
item.description: The largest artificial intelligence data center ever built by Facebook’s parent company Meta is coming to northeast Louisiana, the company said Wednesday, bringing hopes that the $10 billion facility will transform an economically neglected corner of the state. Republican Gov.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Quake prompts brief tsunami warning on the West Coast. Here's what to know about tsunamis
item.description: The powerful earthquake that struck in Northern California on Thursday prompted a brief tsunami warning affecting about 5 million people along a stretch of the West Coast — from Northern California to Oregon — before being canceled.
item.dc:creator: Mark Thiessen, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: This GSA official takes on the dark underside of Artificial Intelligence
item.description: "We need AI tools in place to help us plan around climate events, to help us plan around looking at ways in which we can accommodate," said Dan Pomeroy. The post This GSA official takes on the dark underside of Artificial Intelligence first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: District of Columbia says Amazon secretly stopped fast deliveries to 2 predominantly Black ZIP codes
item.description: The District of Columbia sued Amazon on Wednesday, alleging the company secretly stopped providing its fastest delivery service to residents of two predominantly Black neighborhoods while still charging millions of dollars for a membership that promises the benefit.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: If you had a Patreon account, you may qualify for this payment
item.description: Patreon will pay $7.25 million across users who had accounts and accessed videos on Patreon.com between April 1, 2016 and Sept. 23, 2024.
item.dc:creator: Katie Millard, NewsNation

item.title: OpenAI's Sam Altman 'not that worried' about rival Elon Musk's influence in the Trump administration
item.description: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman, who is in a legal dispute with rival Elon Musk, said he is “not that worried” about Musk's influence in the incoming Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Kyndryl, Nokia expand AI, quantum-safe connectivity options
item.description: Kyndryl is extending its relationship with Nokia and expanding its menu of integration services by including more of Nokia’s data center networking products.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Fortinet launches integrated cloud app security service
item.description: Fortinet has melded some of its previously available services into an integrated cloud package aimed at helping customers secure applications.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Report: AI expands the IT workforce as tech jobs return to offices
item.description: Artificial intelligence is driving growth for the IT workforce, and jobs in the technology sector are stabilizing as the market continues to show moderate growth amidst economic volatility, rapid hiring cycles, and public layoffs, according to a new study by IT staffing firm Motion Recruitment.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: AWS upgrades its 10p10u network to handle massive AI clusters
item.description: While virtualized compute is the foundation of cloud computing, enabling all that compute to transmit data is the job of the network.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Max to begin cracking down on password sharing next week
item.description: Max will begin its password-sharing crackdown next week, beginning with "very early, gentle messaging" directed at high-usage users.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Hallenbeck, NewsNation

item.title: Meta quietly leans on rival GPT-4 despite Zuckerberg’s bold Llama claims
item.description: Mark Zuckerberg has consistently championed Meta’s Llama AI model as a leader in generative AI technology, positioning it as a strong competitor to OpenAI and Google. However, behind the scenes, Meta is complementing Llama with a rival AI model to meet its internal needs.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: How to bulk delete spam and old text messages on iPhone
item.description: Tech wizard Kurt "CyberGuy" Knutsson explains how to delete old unwanted texts and spam messages from cluttered inboxes of an iPhone in bulk.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Microsoft moves to stop M365 Copilot from ‘oversharing’ data
item.description: As Microsoft pushes for businesses to adopt its Microsoft 365 Copilot, many customers have run into a major stumbling block: the AI assistant’s ability to surface confidential information to employees.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Intel’s CEO, Pat Gelsinger, ‘retires’ — riiiiight
item.description: Back in the 1990s, when Bill Clinton was president, people figured out they might just want to be on the internet, and Intel and Microsoft were the major technology powers.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Cython tutorial: How to speed up Python
item.description: Python is a powerful programming language that is easy to learn and easy to work with, but it is not always the fastest to run—especially when you’re dealing with math or statistics.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Intro to Express.js: Endpoints, parameters, and routes
item.description: Express.js is the most-used HTTP server and middleware platform for Node.js. Let’s take a hands-on look at what it brings to the table. Request handling with Express.js Handling requests over the Internet is one of the most frequent and common tasks in software development.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Murder trial of tech consultant in stabbing death of Cash App founder Bob Lee to go to jurors
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Closing arguments in the high-profile murder trial of a tech consultant charged in the stabbing death of Cash App founder Bob Lee concluded Tuesday, with both sides urging jurors to use their common sense to deliver justice.
item.dc:creator: JANIE HAR, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Founder of failed crypto lending platform Celsius Network pleads guilty to fraud charges
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The founder and former CEO of the failed cryptocurrency lending platform Celsius Network could face decades in prison after pleading guilty Tuesday to federal fraud charges, admitting that he misled customers about the business.
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Microsoft hit with more litigation accusing it of predatory pricing
item.description: A UK law firm on Tuesday filed what amounts to a class-action lawsuit against Microsoft, accusing it of using its market share to overcharge clients running Windows Server on AWS, Google, or Alibaba cloud offerings instead of on Microsoft Azure.
item.dc:creator: Network World
